---
title: "Trajectories_GAM_L"
output: html_notebook
author: Karolina Gustavsson
date: 2026-05-14
---

Input data: AMORIS (longitudinal) dvs results_dt från BA_rate_L.rmd

# 1.0 Making figures

## 1.1 Overview GAM curve and spagetti plot 
```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(mgcv)

# Step 1: Filter full data for consistent plot scale
clean_df <- subset(results_dt, age >= 17 & age <= 82)

# Step 2: Subsample 5% of individuals for spaghetti plot
set.seed(42)
subset_ids <- sample(unique(clean_df$sampleID), floor(0.01 * length(unique(clean_df$sampleID))))
spaghetti_data <- clean_df[clean_df$sampleID %in% subset_ids, ]

# Step 3: Fit GAM model to full data
gam_fit <- gam(kdm ~ s(age), data = clean_df)

# Step 4: Predict values for smooth curve (200 evenly spaced age points)
age_seq <- seq(17, 82, length.out = 200)
pred_df <- data.frame(age = age_seq)
preds <- predict(gam_fit, newdata = pred_df, se.fit = TRUE)

pred_df$fit   <- preds$fit
pred_df$upper <- preds$fit + 2 * preds$se.fit
pred_df$lower <- preds$fit - 2 * preds$se.fit

# Step 5: Plot
p <- ggplot() +
  # Tiny dots for observations
  geom_point(data = spaghetti_data, aes(x = age, y = kdm, color = sampleID),
             size = 0.1, alpha = 0.5, show.legend = FALSE) +
  # Spaghetti lines per sampleID
  geom_line(data = spaghetti_data, aes(x = age, y = kdm, group = sampleID),
            color = "#0072B2", alpha = 0.3) +
  # GAM confidence ribbon
  geom_ribbon(data = pred_df, aes(x = age, ymin = lower, ymax = upper),
              fill = "darkred", alpha = 0.4) +
  # GAM fitted line
  geom_line(data = pred_df, aes(x = age, y = fit),
            color = "darkred", size = 1) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(10, 80, by = 10), limits = c(17, 82)) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(10, 100, by = 10), limits = c(17, 100)) +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 1) +
  labs(x = "Chronological Age", y = "KDM") +
  theme_bw()

print(p)
```

```{r}
png("W:/C6_AmorisGuest/Sara Hagg/Karolina Gustavsson/PhD_studies/Study_1/Script/Current_version/Longitudinal/spagetti_kdm_gam1_17_82_1perc.png", 
    width = 4, height = 6, units = 'in', res = 1000)
print(p)  # Send plot to file
dev.off()  # Close device
```

## 1.2 Confidence interval table
```{r}
# 5-year landmark ages (for chronological age)
# Among individuals aged ~X years, what is the estimated mean KDM (± CI) according to the GAM?
landmarks <- seq(20, 80, by = 5)   # or seq(17, 82, by = 5)

landmark_df <- data.frame(age = landmarks)

pr <- predict(gam_fit, newdata = landmark_df, se.fit = TRUE)

landmark_df$fit   <- pr$fit
landmark_df$lower <- pr$fit - 2 * pr$se.fit
landmark_df$upper <- pr$fit + 2 * pr$se.fit

# pretty printing
landmark_df$fit   <- round(landmark_df$fit,   2)
landmark_df$lower <- round(landmark_df$lower, 2)
landmark_df$upper <- round(landmark_df$upper, 2)

print(landmark_df, row.names = FALSE)
```
```{r}
write.csv(landmark_df,
          file = file.path(getwd(), "GAM_ci_per5year.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)
```

## 1.3 Random subsets for the spagetti plots
```{r}
# Packages
library(ggplot2)
library(mgcv)
library(gridExtra)

## ---------- 0) Setup & cleaning ----------
age_col <- if ("prov_age" %in% names(results_dt)) "prov_age" else "age"

keep <- !is.na(results_dt[[age_col]]) & !is.na(results_dt$kdm) &
        results_dt[[age_col]] >= 17 & results_dt[[age_col]] <= 82
clean_df <- results_dt[keep, , drop = FALSE]

uids_all <- unique(clean_df$sampleID)
P_all    <- length(uids_all)

used_ids <- if (exists("subset_ids")) unique(subset_ids) else uids_all[0]
uids_avail <- setdiff(uids_all, used_ids)
P_avail    <- length(uids_avail)

## ---------- 1) Fit GAM ----------
form   <- as.formula(paste0("kdm ~ s(", age_col, ")"))
gam_fit <- mgcv::gam(form, data = clean_df)

age_seq <- seq(17, 82, length.out = 200)
pred_df <- setNames(data.frame(age_seq), age_col)
preds   <- predict(gam_fit, newdata = pred_df, se.fit = TRUE)
pred_df$fit   <- preds$fit
pred_df$upper <- preds$fit + 2 * preds$se.fit
pred_df$lower <- preds$fit - 2 * preds$se.fit

## ---------- 2) 10 disjoint chunks ----------
n_panels    <- 10L
chunk_size  <- max(1L, floor(0.01 * P_all))
if (n_panels * chunk_size > P_avail) {
  chunk_size <- max(1L, floor(P_avail / n_panels))
}

set.seed(42)
uids_shuffled <- sample(uids_avail, P_avail, replace = FALSE)
take_n   <- min(length(uids_shuffled), n_panels * chunk_size)
uids_used <- if (take_n > 0) uids_shuffled[seq_len(take_n)] else uids_shuffled[0]
chunks <- split(uids_used, rep(seq_len(n_panels), each = chunk_size, length.out = length(uids_used)))

## ---------- 3) Plot function (bigger panels, smaller text, fixed blue) ----------
make_spaghetti_plot <- function(ids) {
  d <- clean_df[clean_df$sampleID %in% ids, , drop = FALSE]
  ggplot(data = d, aes_string(x = age_col, y = "kdm")) +
    geom_point(size = 0.005, alpha = 0.5, color = "steelblue") +
    geom_line(aes(group = sampleID), alpha = 0.3, color = "steelblue") +
    geom_ribbon(data = pred_df, aes_string(x = age_col, ymin = "lower", ymax = "upper"),
                inherit.aes = FALSE, fill = "darkred", alpha = 0.35) +
    geom_line(data = pred_df, aes_string(x = age_col, y = "fit"),
              inherit.aes = FALSE, color = "darkred", linewidth = 0.6) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(20, 80, by = 10), limits = c(17, 82)) +
    scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(10, 100, by = 10), limits = c(17, 100)) +
    coord_fixed(ratio = 1) +
    labs(x = "Chronological Age", y = "KDM") +
    theme_bw(base_size = 9) +  # smaller text
    theme(
      axis.title = element_text(size = 9),
      axis.text  = element_text(size = 7),
      plot.title = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )
}
#main settings
#    geom_point(size = 0.2, alpha = 0.5, color = "steelblue") +
#    geom_line(aes(group = sampleID), alpha = 0.3, color = "steelblue") +

#2
#    geom_point(size = 0.1, alpha = 0.5, color = "steelblue") +
#    geom_line(aes(group = sampleID), alpha = 0.3, color = "steelblue") +

#3
#geom_point(size = 0.05, alpha = 0.5, color = "steelblue") +

#4
#geom_point(size = 0.02, alpha = 0.5, color = "steelblue") +

#5
#geom_point(size = 0.005, alpha = 0.5, color = "steelblue") +


## ---------- 4) Arrange ----------
plots <- lapply(seq_len(n_panels), function(i) make_spaghetti_plot(chunks[[i]]))
grid  <- gridExtra::grid.arrange(grobs = plots, ncol = 2)

## ---------- 5) Save (A4) ----------
# Save as PNG in A4 format (portrait, 600 dpi)
png("W:/C6_AmorisGuest/Sara Hagg/Karolina Gustavsson/PhD_studies/Study_1/Script/Current_version/Longitudinal/kdmspag_10grids1percent_5.png",
    width = 8.27, height = 11.69, units = "in", res = 600)

gridExtra::grid.arrange(grobs = plots, ncol = 2)

dev.off()

```


## 1.4 Additional libraries for density version of spagetti plots
```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(mgcv)     # you’re using gam()
library(dplyr)
library(MASS)     # kde2d
library(fields)   # interp.surface
```

## 1.5: Single plot 2D KDE → color segments by local density
```{r}
# Step 1: Filter full data for consistent plot scale
clean_df <- subset(results_dt, age >= 17 & age <= 82)

# Step 2: Subsample 1% of individuals for spaghetti plot (your code uses 0.01)
set.seed(42)
subset_ids <- sample(unique(clean_df$sampleID),
                     floor(0.01 * length(unique(clean_df$sampleID))))
spaghetti_data <- clean_df[clean_df$sampleID %in% subset_ids, ]

# Step 3: Fit GAM model to full data
gam_fit <- gam(kdm ~ s(age), data = clean_df)

# Step 4: Predict values for smooth curve (200 evenly spaced age points)
age_seq <- seq(17, 82, length.out = 200)
pred_df <- data.frame(age = age_seq)
preds <- predict(gam_fit, newdata = pred_df, se.fit = TRUE)

pred_df$fit   <- preds$fit
pred_df$upper <- preds$fit + 2 * preds$se.fit
pred_df$lower <- preds$fit - 2 * preds$se.fit

# ---- Strategy 1: 2D KDE + color segments by local density ----
add_kde_density <- function(df, x = "age", y = "kdm", n = 250) {
  xx <- df[[x]]
  yy <- df[[y]]
  kde <- MASS::kde2d(xx, yy, n = n)

  surf <- list(x = kde$x, y = kde$y, z = kde$z)
  df$dens <- fields::interp.surface(surf, cbind(xx, yy))
  df
}

spag_d <- spaghetti_data %>%
  add_kde_density(x = "age", y = "kdm", n = 250) %>%
  group_by(sampleID) %>%
  mutate(
    xend = lead(age),
    yend = lead(kdm),
    dens_seg = (dens + lead(dens)) / 2
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  filter(!is.na(xend), !is.na(yend), !is.na(dens_seg))

# Step 5: Plot (spaghetti FIRST, red ribbon/line LAST)
p <- ggplot() +
  # Points first (as in your original style)
  geom_point(
    data = spaghetti_data,
    aes(x = age, y = kdm),
    size = 0.1, alpha = 0.5,
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  # Spaghetti segments colored by density
  geom_segment(
    data = spag_d,
    aes(x = age, y = kdm, xend = xend, yend = yend, color = dens_seg),
    linewidth = 0.25,
    alpha = 0.7,
    lineend = "round"
  ) +
  # REMOVE the density legend (label at the side)
  guides(color = "none") +
  # GAM confidence ribbon (on top)
  geom_ribbon(
    data = pred_df,
    aes(x = age, ymin = lower, ymax = upper),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    fill = "darkred",
    alpha = 0.4
  ) +
  # GAM fitted line (last, on top)
  geom_line(
    data = pred_df,
    aes(x = age, y = fit),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    color = "darkred",
    size = 1
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(10, 80, by = 10), limits = c(17, 82)) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(10, 100, by = 10), limits = c(17, 100)) +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 1) +
  labs(x = "Chronological Age", y = "KDM") +
  theme_bw()

print(p)
```

```{r}
png("W:/C6_AmorisGuest/Sara Hagg/Karolina Gustavsson/PhD_studies/Study_1/Script/Current_version/Longitudinal/spagetti_kdm_gam1_17_82_1perc_density.png", 
    width = 4, height = 6, units = 'in', res = 1000)
print(p)  # Send plot to file
dev.off()  # Close device
```

## 1.6 Random subsets for the spagetti plots with density shading
```{r}
# Packages
library(ggplot2)
library(mgcv)
library(gridExtra)
library(dplyr)
library(MASS)
library(fields)

## ----------- 0) Setup & cleaning -----------
age_col <- if ("prov_age" %in% names(results_dt)) "prov_age" else "age"

keep <- !is.na(results_dt[[age_col]]) &
  !is.na(results_dt$kdm) &
  results_dt[[age_col]] >= 17 &
  results_dt[[age_col]] <= 82

clean_df <- results_dt[keep, , drop = FALSE]

uids_all <- unique(clean_df$sampleID)
P_all <- length(uids_all)

used_ids <- if (exists("subset_ids")) unique(subset_ids) else uids_all[0]
uids_avail <- setdiff(uids_all, used_ids)
P_avail <- length(uids_avail)

## ----------- 1) Fit GAM on full cleaned data -----------
form <- as.formula(paste0("kdm ~ s(", age_col, ")"))
gam_fit <- mgcv::gam(form, data = clean_df)

age_seq <- seq(17, 82, length.out = 200)
pred_df <- setNames(data.frame(age_seq), age_col)
preds <- predict(gam_fit, newdata = pred_df, se.fit = TRUE)

pred_df$fit   <- preds$fit
pred_df$upper <- preds$fit + 2 * preds$se.fit
pred_df$lower <- preds$fit - 2 * preds$se.fit

## ----------- 2) Create 10 disjoint ID groups explicitly -----------
n_panels <- 10L
chunk_size <- max(1L, floor(0.01 * P_all))

# if too few available IDs, shrink chunk size
if (n_panels * chunk_size > P_avail) {
  chunk_size <- max(1L, floor(P_avail / n_panels))
}

set.seed(42)
uids_shuffled <- sample(uids_avail, length(uids_avail), replace = FALSE)

panel_ids <- vector("list", n_panels)

for (i in seq_len(n_panels)) {
  start_idx <- (i - 1L) * chunk_size + 1L
  end_idx   <- min(i * chunk_size, length(uids_shuffled))
  
  if (start_idx <= length(uids_shuffled) && start_idx <= end_idx) {
    panel_ids[[i]] <- uids_shuffled[start_idx:end_idx]
  } else {
    panel_ids[[i]] <- character(0)
  }
}

## ----------- 3) Helper: density for segment colouring -----------
add_kde_density <- function(df, x, y, n = 250) {
  xx <- df[[x]]
  yy <- df[[y]]
  
  out <- df
  out$dens <- NA_real_
  
  ok <- is.finite(xx) & is.finite(yy)
  xx_ok <- xx[ok]
  yy_ok <- yy[ok]
  
  if (length(xx_ok) < 2 || length(unique(xx_ok)) < 2 || length(unique(yy_ok)) < 2) {
    return(out)
  }
  
  kde <- MASS::kde2d(xx_ok, yy_ok, n = n)
  surf <- list(x = kde$x, y = kde$y, z = kde$z)
  out$dens[ok] <- fields::interp.surface(surf, cbind(xx_ok, yy_ok))
  
  out
}

## ----------- 4) Plot function -----------
make_spaghetti_plot <- function(ids) {
  
  d <- clean_df[clean_df$sampleID %in% ids, , drop = FALSE]
  
  if (nrow(d) == 0) {
    return(
      ggplot() +
        theme_void()
    )
  }
  
  d <- d[order(d$sampleID, d[[age_col]]), , drop = FALSE]
  d <- add_kde_density(d, x = age_col, y = "kdm", n = 250)
  
  spag_d <- d %>%
    group_by(sampleID) %>%
    mutate(
      xend = lead(.data[[age_col]]),
      yend = lead(kdm),
      dens_seg = (dens + lead(dens)) / 2
    ) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    filter(!is.na(xend), !is.na(yend), !is.na(dens_seg))
  
  ggplot() +
    geom_point(
      data = d,
      aes_string(x = age_col, y = "kdm"),
      size = 0.005,
      alpha = 0.5,
      show.legend = FALSE
    ) +
    geom_segment(
      data = spag_d,
      aes_string(
        x = age_col, y = "kdm",
        xend = "xend", yend = "yend",
        color = "dens_seg"
      ),
      linewidth = 0.25,
      alpha = 0.7,
      lineend = "round",
      show.legend = FALSE
    ) +
    guides(color = "none") +
    geom_ribbon(
      data = pred_df,
      aes_string(x = age_col, ymin = "lower", ymax = "upper"),
      inherit.aes = FALSE,
      fill = "darkred",
      alpha = 0.35
    ) +
    geom_line(
      data = pred_df,
      aes_string(x = age_col, y = "fit"),
      inherit.aes = FALSE,
      color = "darkred",
      linewidth = 0.6
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(20, 80, by = 10), limits = c(17, 82)) +
    scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(10, 100, by = 10), limits = c(17, 100)) +
    coord_fixed(ratio = 1) +
    labs(x = "Chronological Age", y = "KDM") +
    theme_bw(base_size = 9) +
    theme(
      axis.title = element_text(size = 9),
      axis.text = element_text(size = 7),
      plot.title = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )
}

## ----------- 5) Build all 10 plots -----------
plots <- lapply(seq_len(n_panels), function(i) make_spaghetti_plot(chunks[[i]]))

g <- gridExtra::arrangeGrob(grobs = plots, ncol = 2)

# show in RStudio / notebook
grid::grid.newpage()
grid::grid.draw(g)

## ----------- 5) Save (A4) -----------
png("W:/C6_AmorisGuest/Sara Hagg/Karolina Gustavsson/PhD_studies/Study_1/Script/Current_version/Longitudinal/kdmspag_10groups_density_NEeW.png",
  width = 8.27, height = 11.69, units = "in", res = 600
)


grid::grid.newpage()
grid::grid.draw(g)

invisible(dev.off())
```
